verses.text: Woe to 'the bloody city,' full of lies and plunder, heaped with the slain. Violence repaid. A city built on bloodshed reaps a harvest of corpses.
Woe to 'the bloody city,' full of lies and plunder, heaped with the slain. Violence repaid. A city built on bloodshed reaps a harvest of corpses.
Woe to 'the bloody city,' full of lies and plunder, heaped with the slain. Violence repaid. A city built on bloodshed reaps a harvest of corpses.
For her many whoredoms and witchcrafts, God says, 'I am against thee,' and will expose her shame. Seduction judged. The nation that lured others to ruin is publicly disgraced.
For her many whoredoms and witchcrafts, God says, 'I am against thee,' and will expose her shame. Seduction judged. The nation that lured others to ruin is publicly disgraced.
He will cast filth upon her, and all who see shall flee, saying, 'Nineveh is laid waste'; who will bemoan her? Unmourned ruin. The cruel city falls with none to pity her.
He will cast filth upon her, and all who see shall flee, saying, 'Nineveh is laid waste'; who will bemoan her? Unmourned ruin. The cruel city falls with none to pity her.
Was she better than populous No, also carried captive? She too shall be made drunk and hidden. No exemption. The fate of other proud cities awaits her.
Was she better than populous No, also carried captive? She too shall be made drunk and hidden. No exemption. The fate of other proud cities awaits her.
Was she better than populous No, also carried captive? She too shall be made drunk and hidden. No exemption. The fate of other proud cities awaits her.
Her strongholds are like fig trees ready to fall; her soldiers are 'women'; her gates lie open to the foe. Crumbling defences. The boasted bulwarks give way at a touch.
Her strongholds are like fig trees ready to fall; her soldiers are 'women'; her gates lie open to the foe. Crumbling defences. The boasted bulwarks give way at a touch.
Her strongholds are like fig trees ready to fall; her soldiers are 'women'; her gates lie open to the foe. Crumbling defences. The boasted bulwarks give way at a touch.
Draw water and fortify; yet there the fire shall devour and the sword cut off like the cankerworm. Useless labour. No preparation can save the city God has doomed.
Draw water and fortify; yet there the fire shall devour and the sword cut off like the cankerworm. Useless labour. No preparation can save the city God has doomed.
Her merchants are multiplied above the stars, but her crowned ones flee 'as the locusts' when the sun rises. Fleeting wealth. The traders and princes vanish in the day of trouble.
Her merchants are multiplied above the stars, but her crowned ones flee 'as the locusts' when the sun rises. Fleeting wealth. The traders and princes vanish in the day of trouble.

Thy shepherds slumber, O king of Assyria; thy people are scattered, 'there is no healing of thy bruise'; all who hear clap their hands. Incurable fall. The world rejoices at the end of a relentless oppressor.
